body_txt: During an important part of the story, our hero, or just the character in focus, kills someone or something. This death could be popping their murder cherry , or it could just be one of the first kills they think is wrong . Even if it was self-defense and completely justified, they still feel guilt-ridden. They look down in a My God, What Have I Done? moment, shocked at their hands because they just became accomplices in taking a life. Often this leads to a Heroic BSoD particularly if the hero is especially moral or idealistic , like an Ideal Hero or All-Loving Hero . The act itself is usually part of a Batman Grabs a Gun situation with the hero breaking a solemn vow that they swore never to break. May be accompanied with Bloody Hallucinations of Guilt if they begin to hallucinate blood on their hands. This has nothing to do with guiltily washing one's hands of blood, metaphorically or literally—that falls under Scrubbing Off the Trauma . In some cases, murder isn't even required to invoke this trope; a sufficiently broken or driven mad character may gaze upon their hands in this way. This is a Sub-Trope to My God, What Have I Done? — the character in question experiences that guilt while staring horrified at their hands. If this trope is in a character's backstory, it's Sympathetic Murder Backstory . Compare Bleed 'Em and Weep , where the character literally cries after taking life/shedding blood. If a character is contemplating their hands for an entirely different reason, usually drug-related, then see Contemplating Your Hands . If the character thinks what they have done is inherently wrong, they may become The Atoner . If they ultimately decide it was necessary, they may decide I Did What I Had to Do and, in darker settings, start their path down Gaining the Will to Kill . See also Handy Emotional Cues . As a Death Trope , expect the possibility of unmarked spoilers.
